##NAMES=Liudongshengite
##RRUFFID=R180016
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Zn_4_Cr_2_(OH)_12_(CO_3_)·3H_2_O
##LOCALITY=79 mine, Gila Co., Arizona, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Ron Gibbs
##DESCRIPTION=Pinkish hexagonal thin platy crystals, associated with cerussite, malachite, rosasite, hemimorphite, mimetite, wulfenite, fornacite, and rarely linarite and kuksite. This is the type specimen.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R180016
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Zn_3.25_Mg_0.17_Cr_2.58_)(OH)_12_(CO_3_)_1.29_·3H_2_O ; The C content of 2.31(2) wt.% was obtained from an Elemental Combustion System equipped with mass spectrometry.
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 3.1107 b: 3.1107 c: 22.64 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 189.73 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
